_comment: WC2026 Group I, Matchday 2 (MetLife Stadium, NJ). MD1: Norway 4-1 Iraq (Haaland brace); Senegal 1-3 France (lost but competitive). Odds Norway +130 (2.30) / Draw +240 (3.40) / Senegal +210 (3.10); O/U 2.5. Opta 44.7/24.8/30.5. MOST BALANCED game so far -> LOW confidence, no strong read. Open game (both attack), rho -0.05.

meta.stakes: Norway (3 pts, beat Iraq 4-1) can qualify with a win; Senegal (0 pts, lost to France) must respond or risk early exit. Haaland in red-hot form (scored in 11 straight for Norway).

home.name: Norway
notes: Haaland-led attack (brace vs Iraq), red-hot form. attack 1.20 (the 4-1 was partly a weak Iraq).
Decent but not elite defense -> Senegal can score, defense 0.90.
Slight favorite only (~42%): this is a balanced game, calibrated to the market.

away.name: Senegal
notes: Quality, physical African side; lost 1-3 to France but competed and scored. attack 1.15.
Solid defense but Haaland is a handful -> defense 1.03.
Must win after losing to France: will go for it -> open match, rho -0.05.

cards._referee: Wilton Sampaio (Brazil), CONFIRMED. Strict CONMEBOL referee, career ~4.4-5.5 YC/match (high). International discount + bump for a competitive, physical, must-win match -> a HIGH-card game. Over cards is a value angle (book line likely set on an average ref).
